description: Main window. Sidecar execution scoped to the ideaspaces CLI binary only. `args: true` is required: Tauri runs a sidecar with the matched scope entry's args (not the call's), so per-command arg allow-lists all resolve to the first entry. Verbs are passed at the call site instead. Filesystem access is read/write text + read-dir + mkdir scoped to the home tree — clones may live anywhere the user picked (default `~/IdeaSpaces`, or Documents/Dropbox), and the editor reads/writes their note *content* and creates notes/folders only; git stays in the sidecar. macOS TCC gates protected folders on top of this scope. TODO(security, post-v1): tighten `$HOME/**` to the clone dir by routing note read/write through the CLI sidecar (the sidecar already runs in any clone with no fs scope), removing webview home-tree access entirely. `dialog:allow-ask` backs the editor's native unsaved-changes confirmation.
